notes:
nghe nói = “hear that…” / “I’ve heard that…”. It’s used when you got information from someone else, not from direct experience.

It’s a reporting phrase, so it introduces a whole clause after it.  

It implies second-hand information, a bit uncertain.  
Close synonym: nghe bảo (very similar meaning, often slightly more informal).
exampleSentences: Anh nghe nói quán cà phê mới mở rất đẹp và yên tĩnh.
Em nghe nói chị chuẩn bị đi du lịch Đà Nẵng đúng không?
Chị nghe nói có chương trình khuyến mãi lớn cuối tuần này.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I hear the new coffee shop is very beautiful and quiet.
I hear you are about to travel to Da Nang, right?
I hear there is a big promotion this weekend.
